Specimen information

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated creatinine, proteinuria, and abnormal urine findings (cloudy, brown, low specific gravity, bilirubin, ketones), suggesting significant renal impairment with possible nephrotic features. Metabolic acidosis is indicated by low CO₂ and high chloride. HbA1c is unexpectedly low despite glucosuria and ketonuria, which may reflect recent glycemic changes or analytical interference. Urgent nephrology evaluation is recommended to clarify etiology and guide management.
